orchestrator_notes: Agents: read this file BEFORE starting. If orchestrator_directives contains STOP, halt immediately. This audit gates the CE public launch on April 5, 2026. Every agent MUST read the spec document (handovers/CODE_PROVENANCE_LICENSE_AUDIT.md) before starting — it contains the license policy table, edition-aware triage rules, and output format requirements. CRITICAL: Do NOT resolve findings. Do NOT rewrite code. Document everything and stop. If you find ANY AGPL dependency or code match, note it immediately in blockers_encountered — this is a BLOCK for BOTH editions.

sessions.title: Tool Installation + Dependency License Scan
planned_tasks: Create audit/ output directory, verify .gitignore
Install ScanCode Toolkit, SCANOSS, pip-licenses
Run pip-licenses for Python dependency tree
Run license-checker for npm dependency tree
Produce audit/DEPENDENCY_LICENSES.md with flagged items
Early warning check for AGPL dependencies
tasks_completed: audit/DEPENDENCY_LICENSES.md produced with full tables and flagged items
AGPL early warning check: ZERO AGPL dependencies found
deviations: protobuf downgraded to 6.33.6 by scancode-toolkit install (was 5.29.5) — creates incompatibility with google-ai-generativelanguage and grpcio-status. Not a license issue but worth noting for dev env.
sessions.notes_for_next: Tool versions: ScanCode 32.5.0, SCANOSS 1.51.0, pip-licenses 5.5.5, license-checker 25.0.1. Python deps: 268 total (248 permissive, 19 copyleft/mixed, 1 unknown). npm deps: 383 total (381 permissive, 1 MPL dual-licensed, 1 own project). ZERO AGPL dependencies in either ecosystem. All GPL-flagged Python packages are scancode-toolkit/scanoss dependencies (audit tools only, not shipped). Two LGPL project deps to note: psycopg2-binary (has LGPL exception clause — safe) and pycountry (transitive via sumy, dynamic linking — standard usage is safe). MPL deps (certifi, tqdm, dompurify) are all safe — unmodified files or dual-licensed. No dependency-level blockers for CE launch. Source-level scanning (0860b/c) should verify no copyleft code is embedded in project source files.
sessions.summary: Dependency license scan complete. 268 Python + 383 npm packages scanned. ZERO AGPL found. No GPL in shipped dependencies (all GPL packages are audit-tool-only). Two LGPL deps (psycopg2-binary, pycountry) assessed as safe for CE. No blockers for either edition at the dependency level.

Parse and triage results per license policy table

sessions.title: SCANOSS Snippet Scanning
planned_tasks: SCANOSS backend scan (src/giljo_mcp/, api/)
SCANOSS frontend scan (frontend/src/)
Parse and triage with edition-aware rules
Filter expected false positives (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Vue boilerplate)
Produce audit/SCANOSS_FINDINGS.md with all 5 required sections
tasks_completed: SCANOSS backend scan: 132 files (src/giljo_mcp/) + 110 files (api/) -> audit/scanoss_backend.json
Parse and triage: 12 snippet matches, 0 file matches across 384 files
False positive filtering: 7 false positives identified (SQLAlchemy, FastAPI, Vue boilerplate patterns)
audit/SCANOSS_FINDINGS.md produced with all 5 required sections plus false positive analysis and AGPL assessment
deviations: scanoss-py only accepts a single directory argument — ran src/giljo_mcp/ and api/ as separate scans, then merged into scanoss_backend.json
sessions.notes_for_next: SCANOSS scan complete. 384 files scanned (242 backend, 142 frontend). Match breakdown: 372 none, 12 snippet, 0 file. Triage: 0 BLOCK, 0 TRACK, 1 REVIEW (useToast.js 79% match against unlicensed FreshTab — assessed as common Vue pattern but flagged for owner review), 7 false positives (SQLAlchemy/FastAPI/Vue boilerplate), 4 ignored (below thresholds). ZERO AGPL code provenance concerns — two AGPL-component matches (settings.py 14%, DonutChart.vue 4%) were both framework boilerplate false positives. No API rate limiting encountered — free tier was sufficient. Combined with 0860a dependency results (zero AGPL, zero shipped GPL), no code provenance or license issues block CE launch from the SCANOSS perspective.
sessions.summary: SCANOSS snippet scan complete. 384 source files scanned against OSSKB. Zero BLOCK items, zero TRACK items. One REVIEW item (useToast.js 79% match against unlicensed component — common Vue pattern). Two AGPL-component matches were false positives (SQLAlchemy and Vue boilerplate at 14% and 4%). No code provenance concerns for CE or SaaS editions.
